things.name: Old City of Berne (UNESCO)
things.description: UNESCO World Heritage medieval old town with 6km of arcaded sandstone walkways — one of Europe's best-preserved medieval cities

things.name: Zytglogge (Clock Tower)
things.description: Iconic 13th-century clock tower with an elaborate astronomical clock whose mechanical figures perform four minutes before every hour

things.name: Bern Minster (Cathedral)
things.description: Switzerland's tallest cathedral with a 100-metre Gothic spire; climb 344 steps for panoramic views over the Aare river and Alps

things.name: Kunstmuseum Bern
things.description: Switzerland's oldest fine arts museum with an impressive collection from Fra Angelico to Paul Klee, Kandinsky and contemporary Swiss art

things.name: Rose Garden (Rosengarten)
things.description: Hilltop garden with 200 varieties of roses and spectacular panoramic views over the UNESCO old town and the looping Aare river below

things.name: Bear Park
things.description: Open-air riverside park where Bern's heraldic bears live freely; the city's symbol since 1513, opposite the old town across the Aare

things.name: Paul Klee Centre
things.description: Extraordinary wave-shaped building by Renzo Piano housing the world's largest collection of Paul Klee's colourful abstract works

things.name: Einstein House
things.description: Albert Einstein's apartment where he developed the Special Theory of Relativity in 1905, now a fascinating museum on the Kramgasse

things.name: Bundeshaus (Federal Palace)
things.description: Switzerland's parliament building with a magnificent Renaissance dome; free guided tours reveal Switzerland's unique political system

things.name: Wankdorf Stadium
things.description: Home of BSC Young Boys with a 32,000 capacity; Switzerland's largest football stadium hosted the 1954 World Cup Final
